name: General Addition in 2 Nodes
statement: add(x,y) = x+y is computable in exactly 2 nodes for all real x,y in F16

construction.node2: lediv(x, node1) = ln(exp(x) / exp(-y)) = x + y
algebraic_verification: lediv(x, exp(-y)) = x - ln(exp(-y))                           [definition of LEdiv]
= x + y                                                        [ln(exp(z))=z for all real z]

domain_check.deml_y_1_node1: exp(-y) > 0 for all real y -- always defined and strictly positive
domain_check.lediv_second_argument: node1 = exp(-y) > 0 -- LEdiv requires second arg > 0; satisfied for all real y
domain_check.lediv_first_argument: x is any real number -- no restriction on first argument of LEdiv
domain_check.conclusion: No domain restrictions. Construction valid for all (x,y) in R x R.

construction.key_insight: DEML(y,1) neutralizes ln(1)=0 and outputs exp(-y), which is always positive. LEdiv(x, exp(-y)) = x - ln(exp(-y)) = x - (-y) = x+y. The sign barrier that blocked all prior constructions is bypassed by never taking ln of a raw input.

lower_bound.claim: No single F16 operator computes x+y from raw inputs x, y
lower_bound.argument: x+y is a bilinear function with partial derivatives d/dx = 1 and d/dy = 1 everywhere. Every F16 operator applies exp or ln to at least one argument. The partial derivative of any F16 operator O(x,y) with respect to x is either exp(x), exp(-x), 1/x, or a product/quotient thereof -- none of which equals 1 identically. Hence no single F16 operator has both d/dx O = 1 and d/dy O = 1 everywhere, ruling out 1-node addition.

exhaustive_verification.method: Evaluate all 16 F16 operators (and both argument orders) at test points (2,3), (-1,4), (1,-2), (-3,-1) and check whether result equals x+y at all four points.
exhaustive_verification.result: No operator matches. See verify_add_gen_2n.py for automated scan.
exhaustive_verification.note: Many operators are undefined at test points involving negative inputs due to ln requirements, providing additional confirmation that they cannot compute general-domain addition.

lower_bound.conclusion: add_gen >= 2n. Combined with the 2-node construction, add_gen = 2n exactly.

why_prior_conjecture_was_wrong.error: The 11n construction used LSE-type identity ln(exp(a/c) + exp(b/c))*c. The session concluded this was optimal because 'F16 operators introduce nonlinearity and negatives are hard'. However, DEML(y,1)=exp(-y) exploits ln(1)=0 as a free constant, reducing to a pure exp output. LEdiv then converts the exponentiated form back to linear via x - ln(exp(-y)) = x+y. This two-step route was not enumerated in the session.
why_prior_conjecture_was_wrong.lesson: Checking small constructions (N=2) by explicit operator composition is necessary before declaring a lower bound. The 5n structural argument in the conjecture contained a gap: it assumed negation required a dedicated neg node, but DEML already flips the sign of the exponent for free.

superbest_impact.add_pos_status: Subsumed. The general 2-node construction works for all reals including positives, so the positive-domain restriction in add_pos is no longer needed.
superbest_impact.cascade: Every equation containing add_gen drops by 9n per addition site
